macro_segments.title: Concluding Discussion: Entrusting Food with Am Ha'aretz and the Nature of Concern

micro_segments.title: Questioning Whether We Truly Don't Worry About Swapping Food with Am Ha'aretz
Tosefta Taharot: Wife of Chaver Grinding Grain with Wife of Am Ha'aretz—Permitted When Tamei, Prohibited When Tahora
Rabbi Shimon ben Elazar's Stricter View: Concern Even When Tamei Due to Risk of Food Offered by Friend
Gemara's Question: If Wife of Am Ha'aretz Will Steal from Husband to Offer Food, How Can We Trust Her Not to Swap?
Rav Yosef's Response: Wife Rationalizes Offering Food Using Ox-Threshing Principle from Torah

macro_segments.title: Separating Challah and Terumah from Food of Am Ha'aretz While Maintaining Tahara

micro_segments.title: Rav Yossi ben Meshullam and Rav Yochanan: Cannot Separate Challah from Am Ha'aretz's Dough While Tahora
Reason for Restriction: Flour from Am Ha'aretz Is Assumed Tamei; Cohen Will Think Separated Challah Is Tahora
Permitted Alternative: Make Dough from Flour While Tahora, Then Separate Challah in Tahara
Storing Separated Challah in Unusual Vessels (Kefishah or Anchusa) to Prevent Contact
Similar Rules for Terumah from Olives: Cannot Press Already-Crushed Olives; Can Crush Raw Olives While Tahora

macro_segments.title: Accommodating Parnassa: Why We Allow These Separations Despite Halacha Concerns

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan: Allowances Made for Kdei Chayei (Livelihood) of Bread Kneader and Olive Presser
Tzricha: Both Cases Needed—Kneader's Low Profit Margin vs. Presser's Seasonal High Profit But Limited Time
Mechanism: Warning Am Ha'aretz That Touching Challah Will Make It Tevel, Using His Care About Tevel to Prevent Contact
Concern Addressed: Even If Warning Given, How Ensure He Won't Touch? Unusual Vessels Serve as Visual Reminder
Clarification: Even Vessels of Am Ha'aretz That Cannot Become Tamei Are Acceptable

macro_segments.title: Strengthening and Greeting Non-Jews During Shemitah and Year-Round

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Machzikin Yedei Akum Ba'Shvi'it—Strengthening Non-Jews Working Land During Shemitah
Gemara's Initial Question: Can't You Work With Non-Jews on Shemitah? Rav Dimi Says You Cannot Hoe With Them
Anecdotes: Rav Yehuda and Rav Sheshet Said Encouragement to Workers; Rav Chisda Greeted Non-Jews with Shalom
Mishnah's Final Clause: Don't Say Shalom Twice; But Can Ask Their Well-Being on Festivals in Moderate Tone
Rav Geviba's Interpretation: Machzikin Means Only Saying Achzuki (Yasher Koach) Encouragement, Not Actually Working

macro_segments.title: Story of Gneiva: Respecting Learned Non-Jews and Laws of Honoring Animals Before Eating

micro_segments.title: Rav Huna and Rav Chisda Debate Whether to Stand for Gneiva; He Greets Them as Kings with Double Shalom
Gneiva Justifies Address as King: Through Me Kings Have Their Reign—Torah Makes One into a King
Rav Yehuda Amar Rav: Double Shalom to Kings Based on Verse to David
Gneiva Declines Food: Forbidden to Eat Before Feeding One's Animals—Based on Torah Verse about Giving Grass to Animals First

macro_segments.title: Beginning of Sixth Perek: When Husband Can Retract Conditional Get Before Delivery

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: If Husband Says 'Receive This Get for My Wife,' He Can Retract Until Delivered (Wife Did Not Appoint Shaliach)
Mishnah: If Wife Appoints Shaliach Le'Kabbalah to Receive Get, Husband Cannot Retract After Giving It
Mishnah: Husband Can Circumvent by Saying 'Take and Deliver' Instead of 'Receive'—Making Himself Shaliach for Delivery
Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel: Even If Wife Says 'Take for Me,' It Is Interpreted as Receiving; Husband Cannot Retract

macro_segments.title: Gemara's Analysis: Is Holech (Delivery) Like Zachi (Acquisition on Behalf of)

micro_segments.title: Rav Acha bar Rava: Reason for First Case—Shaliach Was Never Appointed for Kabbalah, So Retraction Possible
Question: If Wife Had Made Him Shaliach Le'Kabbalah, Could Second Case (Holech/Deliver) Also Work Immediately?
Inference: If Holech Works When Wife Made Shaliach Le'Kabbalah, Then Holech Is Like Zachi (Possession on Behalf)
Rav Ashi's Objection: Holech Is Not Like Zachi; First Case Needed to Show Receive (Itkabel) Works When Delivered
Resolution: Itkabel (Receive) Is Chidush—Teaches That Receive Means Make Her Receive via Delivery, Even Without Shaliach Status
